meta.status: POPULATED — live web research completed. DO NOT OVERWRITE — populated by research team.
sources: Property Conservation Company — Listed Building Renovation Cost Guide 2024

benchmarks.costPerM2_highQualityListed: £1,800–£2,000/m² for high-quality listed building renovation (Property Conservation Company, 2024)
benchmarks.costPerM2_comprehensiveRestoration: £2,500–£3,500/m² for comprehensive heritage restoration including structural works, full M&E replacement, and fit-out
benchmarks.costPerM2_phaseAOnly: £500–£1,200/m² for Phase A urgent stabilisation only (roof, masonry, weatherproofing)
benchmarks.listedBuildingUplift: +25–40% over standard construction (specialist materials, specialist contractors, conservation officer requirements, programme uplift, hidden defect risk)
benchmarks.contingencyNorm_standard: 20–30% of construction cost — mandatory for heritage projects
benchmarks.contingencyNorm_vacantBuilding: 30% recommended for long-vacant Victorian buildings of uncertain condition
benchmarks.professionalFees: 12–18% of construction cost (architect, structural engineer, QS, heritage consultant)

niCaseStudies.name: Clifton House (Poor House), Belfast
niCaseStudies.majorFunders: National Lottery Heritage Fund, Architectural Heritage Fund, Historic Environment Division NI
niCaseStudies.keyLesson: NLHF as anchor funder across multiple campaigns. Independent charitable trust governance essential. Grade A listing = priority HED NI access.

niCaseStudies.name: The Duncairn Centre for Arts & Culture, Belfast
niCaseStudies.majorFunders: NLHF, SEUPB PEACE IV, Belfast City Council, Arts Council NI
niCaseStudies.restorationCost: ~£3m–£4m estimated (comparable NI projects at this scale)
niCaseStudies.keyLesson: Cross-community narrative unlocks SEUPB funding. NLHF + SEUPB combination is proven in Belfast. Multi-use programming essential.

niCaseStudies.name: Crumlin Road Gaol, Belfast
niCaseStudies.keyLesson: Heritage tourism viable in Belfast. Private commercial operation = max commercial freedom but locked out of future NLHF/AHF capital. Contrast with Fernhill charitable model.

fernhillEstimate.assumedGIA: 500–750 m² gross internal area (typical Victorian country house — 3 storeys, 6–10 principal rooms). Professional survey required to confirm.
fernhillEstimate.assumedCondition: Long-term vacancy with significant water ingress and progressive deterioration

phaseA.description: Urgent stabilisation — roof replacement or repair, chimney stacks, masonry repointing, rainwater goods, emergency weatherproofing, structural survey

phaseB.description: Core restoration — sash windows, lime plasterwork, timber floors, joinery, full M&E replacement, accessibility provisions, fire detection

phaseC.description: Commercial fit-out — professional kitchen, bar/servery, heritage cinema AV, FF&E, external works (car park, walled garden restoration)

fernhillEstimate.caveat: Parametric estimate only. Professional building condition survey by a chartered surveyor with NI listed building experience is the essential first step before any funding application.
